rationale: Refuse done right: says corpus has no statistics, points to coordinator, cites cases only as illustration without inventing numbers.
Grounded statutory deadlines (§27a 5mo, §22 Abs.6 3mo) matching new gold; omits the empirical up-to-5-years the reference gives, so correctness partial.
Same miss as Pro: answers about §20b work-permit delay, not the asylum-seeker 3-month ban (§4 AuslBG); gold §4 not retrieved.
Correctly names the Aufenthaltsberechtigungskarte (§51 AsylG); misses the RWR+ retention nuance and §13.
Cites §3 persecution (relevant) but misses non-refoulement/family anchors (§50/§45a FPG) and adds procedural cooperation as a merits factor.
Answers about document seizure (§38 FPG) instead of the core point: keep the passport but do not contact RB authorities (§7 Abs.2 AsylG).
Grounded on §10 AsylG + §52 FPG return decision/deportation; misses the no-passport exit-document detail.
Refuse failure: gives a confident grounded-looking answer (§7, §45 NAG) where the corpus does not cover EU-mobility of refugees; should have refused.
Grounded on §88 FPG conditions but neutral where the reference stresses difficulty / Landeshauptmann request; correctness partial.
Surfaces the §19/8 NAG practice and FPG §18 despite NAG-19 not in top-5; matches the practical reference.
Strong: states the §20 Abs.1 NAG passport cap, the §19/8 workaround and the practice; matches the new gold.
Cites the gold §20 Abs.3 Z2 StbG exception over the §10 renunciation rule; misses the tactical tips (do not raise exit, 1:2 student time).
Confirms yes via §7 Abs.1 Z1 NAG-DV valid-travel-document framework (gold); grounded.
